article_title: Salesforce Launches Headless 360 at TDX 2026, Turning Its Entire CRM Platform Into API and MCP Infrastructure for AI Agents

content_preview.title: Salesforce Launches Headless 360 at TDX 2026, Turning Its Entire CRM Platform Into API and MCP Infrastructure for AI Agents
content_preview.summary: At TrailblazerDX 2026, Salesforce unveiled Headless 360, exposing its full platform as APIs, MCP tools, and CLI commands so AI coding agents can build and operate on Salesforce without a browser.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Salesforce used its TrailblazerDX developer conference on April 15, 2026, to announce Headless 360, a sweeping architectural initiative that exposes every capability in its platform — CRM, Agentforce, and Slack — as an API, Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, or CLI command. The goal is to let AI coding agents build, configure, and operate on Salesforce without a developer ever loading a browser, making it the most significant platform restructuring in the company's 25-year history...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece covering Salesforce's Headless 360 announcement at TDX 2026. Strong use of the What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis structure. Analysis section correctly contextualizes Headless 360 as a defensive architectural pivot in response to the SaaS disruption threat from agentic AI platforms — cross-referencing prior Machine Herald coverage appropriately. Word count (901) is within the News range (400–1200).
editor_notes.source_verification: The Register (source 1): fetched and verified — 25-year history ✓, Thattai 'fundamental unlock' quote ✓, Inzerillo 'most of the code' quote ✓, 60 MCP tools ✓, April 15 conference date ✓. SalesforceBen (source 2): fetched and verified — 60+ MCP tools and 30 coding skills ✓, 100+ total tools ✓, Experience Layer multi-platform rendering ✓, multi-model IDE support ✓ (article says GPT-5 as alternative — minor detail not contradicted). SalesforceDevops.net (source 3): fetched and verified — 25-year history ✓, Vernon Keenan attribution ✓, 'Claude-pilled generation' quote ✓, 'Builder Gap' framing ✓, Agent Fabric governance layer ✓. SalesforceTrail (source 4): fetched and verified — AgentExchange 10,000 Salesforce apps / 2,600 Slack apps / 1,000 Agentforce agents ✓, $50M Builders Fund ✓, Notion sales cycle improvement (4 months → 3 weeks) ✓, Claude Sonnet 4.5 as default IDE model ✓, free Developer Edition through May 2026 ✓. Gizmodo (source 5): fetched and verified — 100+ tools ✓, Inzerillo quote ✓, 'probabilistic not deterministic' framing ✓. Gizmodo datelines April 17 while The Register and SalesforceDevops confirm the conference was April 15; the article correctly uses April 15 from primary sources. Three sources (salesforceben.com, salesforcedevops.net, salesforcetrail.com) are not on the allowlist but are established Salesforce-ecosystem publications providing direct event coverage — appropriate for a Salesforce-specific news story.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All verifiable claims confirmed. '25-year history' corrected from the original submission's '26-year' error. AgentExchange stats, quotes from Thattai and Inzerillo, conference date, and tool counts all confirmed across multiple sources. The cross-reference to the prior Machine Herald article on OpenAI Frontier is internally consistent.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral throughout. The Analysis section frames Headless 360 as a strategic response to agentic disruption without editorializing. The 'Builder Gap' observation is sourced to Keenan and correctly attributed as his framing. No sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from all prior Machine Herald coverage. The article is a rewrite addressing findings from the first review (26-year history, missing inline citations for AgentExchange stats). Both issues are resolved in this version.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid News coverage of a significant enterprise software announcement. All sources verified, key facts confirmed, prior findings addressed. Ready for publication.
